description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration

Discovered while implementing bw mutagen-wrd.15. That ticket added __block__-wrapper match clauses to lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex; they are correct and verified end-to-end (test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs "literal __block__ wrapper end-to-end"). But the hypothesis that Code.string_to_quoted(..., token_metadata: true) wraps atomic literals in {:__block__, meta, [value]} does NOT hold in Elixir 1.19.5 for the literals in test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/decisions.ex.

Those literals (0 in n > 0, 1 in do: 1, true/false in boolean operands, 0/1 in case clause heads [0] -> :zero) appear as BARE children of operator / clause-head tuples and carry no metadata of their own. node_line/1 returns nil for them; the enumerator at lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex:224 filters them out (is_nil(line) -> acc).

## Resolution paths

1. Parent-line-inheritance in the enumerator. When prewalk descends from a 3-tuple parent into a literal child, propagate the parents :line via the prewalk accumulator (or a Macro.traverse/4 rewrite). The literal mutators match?(bare_value) then has its coverage check answered by the parent line.

2. Pre-pass that wraps bare literals in __block__. Normalize the parsed AST before enumeration so every bare boolean / 0/1/-1 literal becomes {:__block__, [line: parent_line], [v]}. Downstream code sees the wrapper unconditionally.

## Verification once landed

The e2e Scenario 2a @tag :skip in test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs (line ~425) gets dropped; mix test --only literal_floor_scenario must produce at least one literal site on decisions.ex.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.15 (DISCOVERY-E first pass; __block__ wrapper match clauses)
- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 e2e, where the floor was first surfaced)
- mutagen-wrd.6 (mutation enumerator; this fix lives there)

IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea

stage worktree /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-enumerator-parent-line-inheritance,
based on epic/mutagen-wrd @ 0f4a197)

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_1_arith.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_2_decisions.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_3_result_tuples.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_3b_guarded.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_3c_withblock_with_swap.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_3d_withblock_else_removal.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_4_infinite_looper.json
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_8_macro_holder.json

Tests added (test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs, new describe
"bare-literal parent-line inheritance (bw mutagen-wrd.16)"):
- "bare 0 in a covered comparison produces a literal site on the comparison's line"
- "bare 1 in a covered `do: 1` keyword-shorthand body produces a literal site"
- "bare literal whose ambient line is UNCOVERED produces no site (r2 still wins)"
- "bare 0 in a case clause head inherits the clause head's line"
- "determinism: two consecutive runs with bare literals produce identical site lists"

Tests changed:
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:422 "ordering: scope records are
  walked in input order" — narrowed pattern-match from total sites to arith-only
  filter so the cross-scope ordering check stays load-bearing now that bare
  literals also land sites.
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs:413 Scenario 2a — `@tag :skip` removed,
  describe text + inline comment updated to reflect bw mutagen-wrd.16 landing.

Verification stubs flipped: none — the spec's stubs (v1, v2, v3 in
.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md) were already `execute: true`. The
ticket Verification section's load-bearing check is `mix test --only
literal_floor_scenario` which now passes (1 test, 0 failures) and which
asserts at least one literal site on decisions.ex.

Choice documented (per ticket): picked Resolution path 1 (parent-line-
inheritance in the enumerator) over path 2 (pre-pass `__block__` wrapper).
Rationale recorded inline in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex's
`walk_body/5` doc comment: keeps the AST cache byte-identical to what the
runner restores (r6 invariant), is localised to the walker, and composes
with the literal mutator's existing bare + `__block__` clauses from bw
mutagen-wrd.15.

Runner-side fix: bare-literal sites have an `original_ast` of `0`/`1`/`true`
etc. — no metadata of their own — so the runner's existing
`Macro.prewalk` + `node_matches_site?/2` (which keys off 3-tuple meta)
could not locate them. Added `replace_bare_site/2` as a second-pass walker
that mirrors the enumerator's ambient threading. Runs only when the 3-tuple
pass fails, so the happy-path cost stays at one Macro.prewalk.

Goldens regenerated (REGEN=1 mix test --only e2e_slow): eight scenario
goldens drifted purely additively — each gained one or more literal-site
result entries that the enumerator was previously dropping. Verified the
drift was strictly literal-mutator additions by inspecting diffs and
counting `select(.mutator == "literal")` in old vs new golden via jq.

Verification commands run, all green:
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
- `mix test`: 1 doctest, 347 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded as e2e_slow)
- `mix test --include e2e_slow`: 1 doctest, 360 tests, 0 failures, 1 skipped
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs --only literal_floor_scenario`:
  1 test, 0 failures (12 excluded). Confirms the ticket's named verification.
- `make smoke`: clean

Deferred must requirements: none. Every requirement of
mutagen.mutation_enumeration is still satisfied; r1 (determinism) is
preserved by the new walker's left-to-right pre-order discipline (covered
by the new determinism test + existing s1 tests + the property test). r2
(coverage filter before validate) is preserved (covered by the new
"uncovered ambient line" test and existing s2 tests). r6 (no source
re-reads, AST byte-equal between enumeration and restore) is preserved
because the walker reads from the cache only and never mutates the AST.

Summary: replaced the enumerator's `Macro.prewalk`-based traversal with a
hand-rolled pre-order walker that threads an ambient `{line, column}`
tuple downward, so bare atomic literals (the `0` in `n > 0`, the `1` in
`do: 1`, case-clause-head `0`/`1`) inherit the nearest enclosing 3-tuple's
positional metadata and survive the coverage filter at
mutation_enumerator.ex:310-314. The mutation_runner gained a matching
bare-literal find-and-swap pass so the sites can actually be located and
mutated. End-to-end, `mix mutagen` against decisions.ex now produces 7
literal sites where it previously produced 0; the e2e Scenario 2a
`@tag :skip` is gone.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-a7fa37b440801b6f7 (subagent of eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea/subagents/agent-a7fa37b440801b6f7.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 12 (2 lib, 2 test, 8 goldens)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 — the ticket lacks an explicit
  "Allowed touches" / "Out of Scope (files)" block. Files map to the
  Advances subject's surface (mutation_enumerator.ex) + the ticket's
  named Verification surface (end_to_end_test.exs + literal_floor
  scenario) + the implementer's documented runner-side adapter
  (mutation_runner.ex, see Notes) + enumerator unit tests + the 8
  goldens listed in the IMPLEMENTED comment.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (none defined)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  - v1 (r1/r4/r5): mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs
    → 20 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 (r1): mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs
    → 3 tests, 0 failures
  - v3 (r2/r3): mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs --only filtering
    → 3 tests, 0 failures (17 excluded)
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement on
  mutagen.mutation_enumeration, the only Advances subject):
  - r1 (determinism): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:39 "same inputs produce
    byte-identical site lists across two runs",
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:64 "determinism holds
    across 10 consecutive runs", and the new
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:733 "determinism: two
    consecutive runs with bare literals produce identical site lists".
    Would fail if the hand-rolled walker's child-visit order diverged
    from left-to-right depth-first (the new test compares struct
    equality of r1 and r2, would assert false).
  - r2 (coverage before validate): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:89 (existing) and the
    new test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:669 "bare literal
    whose ambient line is UNCOVERED produces no site". Would fail if
    parent-line-inheritance bypassed the covered_lines filter — the new
    test asserts literal_sites length == 1 with line==2 and zero
    literal_skips when line 3's bare `0` shouldn't site.
  - r3 (skip tracking): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:190,223. Untouched by
    this diff. Would fail if validate-rejected sites silently appeared
    in the main sites list.
  - r4 (multi-defmodule sibling isolation): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:260. Would fail if the
    new walk_tree/6 descended out of the in-scope module's body — the
    test asserts every emitted site's AST hash is from Mod.A's subtree.
  - r5 (behaviour-only module warns): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:320. Would fail if the
    new walker silently produced sites for `@callback`-only modules.
  - r6 (no source re-reads, cache-only): covered by
    test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:362 "enumeration does
    not call File.read or Code.require_file" (uses a nonexistent path
    in the AST cache; would crash with File.Error if anything re-read).
    Confirmed the new walker reads only from arguments — no File or
    Code.require_file calls in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex.
- Intent-level scope concerns:
  - mutation_runner.ex edit (build_mutated_file_ast/2 + new
    replace_bare_site/walk_bare/descend_bare/walk_bare_list/
    update_ambient_runner/bare_literal_site?/bare_match? helpers):
    inspected line-by-line. It is a pure AST find-and-swap adapter
    invoked as a second pass ONLY when the existing 3-tuple meta match
    fails AND the site's original_ast is a bare integer/boolean.
    It does NOT touch: classification (r5 outcomes), restore
    (Code.compile_quoted on cached AST, r6), timeouts (r4/r7), self-
    mutation refusal (r3), baseline (r1/r2), state-drift warnings
    (r8/r9), test reload behaviour (r10), or filesystem (r11). It runs
    on an AST already in memory and returns either {:ok, new_ast} or
    {:error, :site_not_found}, exactly matching the existing
    contract. Verdict: justified runtime adapter for the new enumerator
    output shape, not a behavioural expansion of mutation_pipeline.
- Project verification:
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean (verified via make smoke)
  - mix test: 1 doctest, 347 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded e2e_slow
  - mix test --include e2e_slow: 1 doctest, 360 tests, 0 failures, 1 skipped
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs --only literal_floor_scenario
    (the ticket's named load-bearing check): 1 test, 0 failures, 12 excluded
  - make smoke: clean
- Golden drift: strictly additive. Spot-checked all 8 regenerated goldens
  with jq grouping mutator counts old-vs-new. Every old (arith,
  boolean, compare, case_drop, else_removal) mutator-row count is
  UNCHANGED in the new golden. Only `literal` rows are added (zero
  old, one or more new). Specific tallies:
  - scenario_1_arith: literal 0→1; arith 4→4; total 5→6
  - scenario_2_decisions: literal 0→7; arith 0→0; total 8→15 (this is
    the ticket's headline result — 7 new literal sites on decisions.ex)
  - scenario_3_result_tuples: literal 0→1; arith/case_drop unchanged
  - scenario_3b_guarded: literal 0→1 (survived); boolean/compare unchanged
    (kill_rate dips 1.0→0.667 — legitimate, the new literal at
    lib/lane_fixture/guarded.ex:12 col 49 survives; not masking
    pre-existing regression in other mutators)
  - scenario_3c/3d: literal 0→1; else_removal unchanged
  - scenario_4_infinite_looper: literal 0→3; arith/case_drop/compare unchanged
  - scenario_8_macro_holder: literal 0→1; arith unchanged
  No `:warnings` count drift on non-literal results in spot checks.
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none — no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE
  comments on this ticket.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- No "Allowed touches" / "Out of Scope (files)" block in the ticket
  description. Scope was judged against (a) the Advances subject's
  declared surface (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex), (b) the
  ticket's named Verification surface, and (c) defensible runtime
  support required to make the new enumerator output usable. The
  implementer documented the runner-side adapter rationale inline at
  lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:399-412 and in the IMPLEMENTED
  comment. A stricter reading of "Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration"
  would have routed the runner edit through a separate ticket against
  mutagen.mutation_pipeline; future tickets in this lane should
  consider including explicit Allowed-touches when implementation
  spans multiple spec surfaces.
- The ticket's two-tuple shape comment for keyword pairs is honoured
  by walk_tree/6's `{a, b}` clause (lines 222-227) and mirrored on the
  runner side via descend_bare/3's two-tuple clause (lines 510-514) —
  symmetric handling, important for keyword-shorthand `do: 1` sites.
- mutation_runner.ex:439 reads `node == site.original_ast` for the
  3-tuple primary match. The bare-literal sites' `original_ast` is a
  raw integer/boolean, so a 3-tuple primary scan never matches them
  (Macro.prewalk visits 3-tuples and walks into children but the
  callback's `node` is each node it visits including atoms — the
  guard on node_matches_site?/2 requires `{_kind, meta, _args}` shape,
  so bare atoms fall through). The second-pass guard
  `bare_literal_site?(site)` correctly gates the new path; non-bare
  sites that legitimately fail the primary match still return
  `{:error, :site_not_found}` (no regression in error reporting).

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit, orchestrated)
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage (3/3), full requirement
coverage for r1-r6 with concrete Would-fail-if lines including determinism and
coverage-before-validate pins for the new bare-literal walker path. The
cross-surface mutation_runner.ex edit was scrutinized line-by-line and confirmed
as a pure adapter (no behavioural impact on classification/restore/timeouts/
self-mutation/baseline/state-drift/filesystem). Golden drift independently
verified as strictly additive (mutator-row counts unchanged except new literal
rows; scenario_2 decisions.ex gains 7 literal sites matching the ticket's
headline goal). Project verification clean (347 tests, 0 failures). No auditor
was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: DISCOVERY: Literal mutator floor on decisions.ex needs enumerator parent-line-inheritance
